# RefactorKit v0.5.0 Release Notes

RefactorKit `v0.5.0` publishes the cross-platform runtime, production Build Model
SPI, distribution-trust baseline, and first bounded multi-language kernel. API
baseline remains `0.2`; this release does not claim the final multi-language
`1.0` contract.

## Highlights

- Native self-contained `jlink` runtime assets for Linux x86_64, Windows x86_64,
  macOS x86_64, and macOS arm64, each built and transaction-tested natively.
- Offline Maven effective-reactor and bounded non-executable Gradle declarative
  Build Model providers with module/source-set/generated-root ownership,
  classpath/source-level evidence, typed failures, bounded redacted protocol DTOs,
  and pre-apply drift detection.
- Java 21 Maven reactor analysis and rename-only `moveSourceRoot` with byte-identical
  preview/apply/WAL/rollback and case-folded collision refusal.
- Distribution archive verification for checksum/name binding, traversal,
  symlink, duplicate/case-fold paths, reproducible timestamps, permissions,
  `jlink` modules, native architecture, and launcher execution before publication.
- Deterministic multi-language adapter registry with mixed-language routing,
  capability stability/evidence/mutation authority, and fail-closed evidence
  validation.
- Bounded external semantic process lifecycle: explicit environment, executable
  and argument provenance hashes, stream limits, request deadlines, cancellation,
  natural-exit cleanup, and graceful/forced process-tree termination.
- Source-only semantic workspace overlays plus strict standard LSP WorkspaceEdit
  parsing and normalization. External proposals cannot bypass `PatchPlan`, staged
  diagnostics, approval, `PatchEngine`, WAL, or rollback.
- Real packaged Tree-sitter JNI TypeScript and JavaScript grammars for all runtime
  targets, with bounded native outline/identifier parsing and explicit fallback
  classification for unsupported grammars.
- Language capability schema v1 exposed by CLI, daemon, LSP experimental
  capabilities, and MCP initialization.

## Compatibility and migration

See `docs/releases/v0.5.0-migration.md`. API remains `0.2`; existing documented
Java beta-contract commands remain compatible. New capability fields and methods
are additive. Build model, external semantic process, Tree-sitter, and
multi-language schemas remain pre-1.0 evolution surfaces as classified in the
integration contract.

## Artifacts

The release workflow replaces the placeholders below in the published body.

- Source tag: _TBD before release_
- Release commit: _TBD before release_
- Runtime assets: `refactorkit-runtime-0.5.0-<platform>.zip`
  - Final URL: _TBD after release artifact is published_
  - Final SHA-256: _TBD after release artifact is built_
- Per-platform checksum: `*.zip.sha256`
- Per-platform SBOM: `refactorkit-runtime-<platform>.spdx.json`
- Attestations: GitHub artifact build provenance and SBOM attestations
- macOS signing/notarization: unsigned
- Windows Authenticode: unsigned

## Acceptance

- [x] full build and protocol suites;
- [x] 23/23 golden cases;
- [x] native packaged runtime and managed transaction smoke on all four targets;
- [x] Maven reactor and `moveSourceRoot` apply/WAL/rollback acceptance;
- [x] hostile runtime archive verification;
- [x] bounded semantic process, timeout, stream overflow and process-tree tests;
- [x] source overlay and untrusted WorkspaceEdit normalization tests;
- [x] packaged native TypeScript Tree-sitter smoke;
- [x] read-only Magrathea Java 21 acceptance with zero diagnostics;
- [ ] tag release workflow, final checksums, SBOM/attestations and independent
  downloaded-asset verification (completed by publication).

## Known limitations

- API `0.2` remains beta; stable API `1.0` is deliberately deferred until the
  approved deep multi-language roadmap is complete.
- TypeScript and JavaScript currently have structural Tree-sitter capability,
  not stable semantic mutation authority. TSX/JSX use documented fallback.
- `ExternalLspAdapter` is an internal proposal-provider foundation, not a stable
  end-user TypeScript/JavaScript adapter. Its source-only overlay is not an OS
  sandbox.
- Authenticated/private Maven repositories and executable Gradle providers remain
  unavailable pending separate privileged-provider implementation and acceptance.
- macOS assets are not Developer ID signed/notarized. Windows assets are not
  Authenticode signed; checksums and attestations do not replace code signing.
- Generated sources remain analysis-only unless an operation explicitly proves a
  safe generated-source policy.

## Verification

```bash
sha256sum -c refactorkit-runtime-0.5.0-<platform>.zip.sha256
python3 scripts/verify-runtime-archive.py \
  refactorkit-runtime-0.5.0-<platform>.zip \
  refactorkit-runtime-0.5.0-<platform>.zip.sha256 \
  --platform <platform>
```

Managed workspace changes remain rollbackable:

```bash
refactorkit patch rollback <transaction-id> --root <workspace>
```

Published tags are immutable. A release defect is corrected by a new patch
version, never by moving `v0.5.0`.
